findings.note: run-all.sh executes under set -e and rollback-1.5.0.tests.sh (array position 22) fails on the current tree for a documented pre-existing defect, so a full run-all pass aborts before reaching the newly registered guard-ps1-ascii.tests.sh (last array entry) and the guard-r10-port pwsh block. Registration satisfies AC-015's wiring clause and the failure is loud/fail-closed (nothing ships undetected), but the new suite entries remain latent in end-to-end run-all until the pending rollback fix lands; AC-015 evidence currently rests on the direct-invocation logs.

findings.ref: tests/guard-r10-port.tests.ps1 plugin.json corpus vs bundle manifest header
findings.note: The three ProtectedGatePluginJsonSuffixes entries are each exercised in one path form (2 relative-with-prefix, 1 absolute), not both forms per entry as the bundle header's 'relative and absolute forms' phrasing could suggest; the bare-relative TrimStart('/') matching branch of Test-IsProtectedGateFile is never the deciding branch for any fixture, and no copilot-emit scenario covers the forgery denial (only R-10 denials). Branch/entry coverage still satisfies RT-20260712-002's expected fix and the spec's per-suffix-class bar.

Bundle sanitization over-redacts source content ([REDACTED]/[PATH_REDACTED] replacing $ShellUnsafeTokenChars, $ShellRedirectTokenRe, list initializations, and the Approval/Impl-Review-Status regex literals), so the artifact text cannot be hash-verified against the declared sha256 and a few load-bearing patterns are not directly reviewable; parity for those paths is instead evidenced behaviorally by the 58-scenario RED(12/46)/GREEN(58/0) differential.

Internal inconsistency: the report header records 'Task Attempt Count: 2' while the Iteration And Escalation section still says 'Task Attempt Count: 1' (stale attempt-1 value retained alongside the appended Attempt 2 section).
